meta.title: Cognito Auth POC
meta.description: Proof of concept for authentication with Next.js, NextAuth, and Amazon Cognito.

placeholders.emailCode: Enter the code from your email

status.noExplicitRole: No explicit role found. Default authenticated access is applied.

validation.passwordMin: Password must be at least 8 characters
validation.newPasswordMin: New password must be at least 8 characters

errors.userNotConfirmed: This account has not been confirmed yet
errors.accountAlreadyExists: This email is already in use
errors.invalidPassword: The password does not meet the policy requirements
errors.mfaRequired: This account requires additional MFA verification
errors.newPasswordRequired: This account must set a new password before signing in
errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.userNotFound: User not found in this Cognito user pool
errors.loginUnavailable: Unable to connect to the sign-in service
errors.accessControlUnavailable: Unable to connect to the access-control service

authShell.flowGuideDescription: Complete this step to continue the Cognito journey.

login.panelDescription: This project is a proof of concept for authentication with Next.js, NextAuth, and Amazon Cognito.
login.title: Access your account
login.description: This project is a POC for authentication using Next.js, NextAuth, and Amazon Cognito.

register.title: Create your account
register.description: Register a new Cognito account to start the auth flow.
register.panelDescription: Fill the form, confirm the email, then sign in.

highlights.step1Description: Name is optional. Email and password are required.
highlights.step2Description: Submit the form to create the Cognito user.
highlights.step3Description: Use the code from email before the first login.

forgotPassword.title: Request a reset code
forgotPassword.description: Enter the email attached to the account and the app will move you to the password reset step with that address prefilled.
forgotPassword.panelTitle: Recover the account without breaking the auth flow.
forgotPassword.panelDescription: This step requests a code from Cognito. The next screen uses that code to set a brand-new password.

highlights.step1Description: The email must match an existing Cognito user in the current user pool.
highlights.step2Description: Cognito usually sends the verification code by email right after this request succeeds.
highlights.step3Description: The reset page receives the same email automatically through the query string.

notes.step1: Submit the email to request a reset code from Cognito.
notes.step2: Use that code on the next screen to choose a new password.

confirmAccount.title: Verify your email address
confirmAccount.description: Submit the confirmation code from your inbox to activate the account before the first login.
confirmAccount.panelDescription: This step confirms the Cognito sign-up and completes the path from registration to the first authenticated session.

highlights.step1Description: If you came from sign-up, the account email can already be filled in for you.
highlights.step2Description: Use the latest code sent by email. Old codes may return mismatch or expired errors.
highlights.step3Description: A successful confirmation redirects straight back to the login screen with the email preserved.

notes.checkInbox: Check the inbox for the sign-up confirmation code from Cognito.

resetPassword.title: Choose a new password
resetPassword.description: Enter the verification code from email and set the new password that will be used for the next sign-in.
resetPassword.panelTitle: Finish the recovery flow with one final step.
resetPassword.panelDescription: This screen confirms the reset code with Cognito, replaces the old password, and sends the user back to login.

highlights.step1Description: The reset code must be the latest code sent for this account.
highlights.step2Description: Use a password that matches the rules enforced by the user pool and the local validation.
highlights.step3Description: On success, the flow routes back to login so the new credentials can be tested immediately.

notes.instruction: Paste the code exactly as received, then choose a fresh password.

account.title: Account center and role control
account.description: This is the landing page after a successful login. It shows account information, the current roles, and lets admins manage Cognito group roles from one place.

overview.title: Account overview
overview.authenticatedLandingDescription: Users who sign in successfully are sent to

accessBehavior.title: Access behavior
accessBehavior.canManage: This account can manage other users' roles through Cognito groups.
accessBehavior.cannotManagePrefix: This account can view account information but cannot change roles yet. It must belong to the

accessControl.emptyState: Load a user by email to inspect current Cognito groups and assign the managed roles for this application.
accessControl.noGroups: This user is not in any Cognito groups.

settingsAccessControl.title: Legacy access-control page
settingsAccessControl.description: This page still works for the older flow, but the new center for account details and role changes is the account page.

permissionsGuide.title: How to change permissions
permissionsGuide.step1: Set COGNITO_USER_POOL_ID and AWS credentials for the server before using the role-assignment page.
permissionsGuide.step2: Change the post-login landing page from DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATED_PATH.
permissionsGuide.step3: Set different home paths per role in ROLE_HOME_PATHS.
permissionsGuide.step4: Define which roles can access each route in ROLE_ROUTE_RULES.

roleHomePaths.title: Role home paths
roleHomePaths.empty: No per-role home override. All authenticated users land on /account.

managedRoleGroups.title: Managed Cognito role groups
managedRoleGroups.description: You can change this list with the COGNITO_ROLE_GROUPS environment variable, for example admin,manager,user,auditor.
